rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by ensuring all property definitions and constants are properly declared in the AADL model or its dependencies before use in AGREE contracts.
Resolve type errors in AGREE expressions by ensuring all identifiers and helper declarations refer to valid AADL features or previously declared AGREE constants/variables with matching types.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, only reference AADL features, constants, and types that are visible and properly declared in the current scope or its dependencies.
For type safety in AGREE, ensure that all expressions used in guarantees and assumptions match the expected scalar types: int, real, or bool.
Validate that all property associations and references in AADL models are resolvable before generating AGREE contracts to avoid runtime diagnostics.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and placed in valid component type or implementation scopes.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model in AGREE expressions; avoid referencing implementation-only subcomponents or undefined features.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new property definitions or constants in AGREE annexes that are not already declared in the AADL model or its configured dependencies.
Avoid placing AGREE clauses in invalid scopes such as implementation-level bindings when component type contracts are required.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
